sections.paragraph:
φροντιστής, οῦ, ὁ (s. φροντίζω; X., Pla. et al.; IG XIV, 715; 759; pap [oft. as a t.t. for ‘guardian’]; Philo, Somn. 2, 155) protector, guardian w. objective gen. (Jewish ins fr. Side in Pamphylia: JHS 28, 1908, 195f, no. 29 φρ. τῆς συναγωγῆς) σὺ αὐτῶν φρ. ἔσο IPol 4:1.—DELG s.v. φρήν II 4.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

oft. oft. = often

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

gen. gen. = genitive

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. fr. = from

JHS JHS = Journal of Hellenic Studies—List 6

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1066
